content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed on region-level split. My exact insertion points: (1) cache.py imports: add 'import gzip', 'import pickle', 'import zlib' as new top-level import lines (I will NOT touch the 'from cachetools import LRUCache' line, so you can freely change it to add TTLCache). (2) Cache.__init__ signature: I append 3 new trailing params right after memory_max_entries: 'compress: Optional[str] = None, cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,'. Please add your 'ttl' param AFTER mine (i.e. last param in the list) once you see my DONE, so we don't edit the same trailing comma line simultaneously. (3) __init__ body: I add 'self._compress = compress' near the other self.enable_* assignments, and pass cull_limit/eviction_policy into the FanoutCache(...) call as new kwargs (only when not None). I won't touch the memory_cache branch (LRUCache) - that's yours. (4) get()/put(): I only add header-detect/decompress logic in get() and header/compress logic in put(), both scoped strictly to the enable_disk_cache branches, wrapping the existing 'self.disk_cache[key] = value' and 'response = self.disk_cache[key]' lines. If your TTL put/get logic (disk_cache.set(expire=ttl), disk_cache.expire()) also touches those same lines, let's sequence: I'll finish+DONE first, then you layer expire= handling around my compress/decompress calls without removing them. (5) New function: I add 'set_compression(codec)' immediately after the Cache class ends, right before 'def request_cache('. Please add your 'set_ttl()' at the true bottom of the file (after request_cache's decorator/closures), so our new functions don't land on the same line. (6) __init__.py: I append 'compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy' (all Optional, default None) as new trailing params to configure_cache(), passed as keyword args into Cache(...) (I'm converting that call to use keyword args, which should make it easy for you to append ttl=ttl there too without positional-order issues). I'll import 'set_compression' alongside Cache in the 'from dspy.clients.cache import ...' line and add it to __all__. Going first now - will send DONE shortly with the diff summary so you can layer on top.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementation complete and tested (existing tests/clients/test_cache.py + tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py: 15 passed). Summary of exact changes so you can layer on top:

cache.py:
- New imports: 'import gzip', 'import pickle', 'import zlib' (didn't touch cachetools import line - safe for you to add TTLCache there).
- New module constants: _MAGIC_HEADER=b'DSPC', _CODEC_NONE/_CODEC_GZIP/_CODEC_ZLIB, _COMPRESS_NAME_TO_CODEC dict.
- Cache.__init__ signature: added trailing params 'compress: Optional[str] = None, cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,' right after memory_max_entries (before the closing paren) - please append your 'ttl: Optional[int] = None,' right after eviction_policy as the new last param.
- __init__ body: added validation + 'self._compress = compress' right after 'self.enable_memory_cache = enable_memory_cache' (before the memory_cache if/else - your TTLCache branch is untouched). FanoutCache(...) call now builds a 'disk_cache_settings' dict conditionally including cull_limit/eviction_policy and passes it via **disk_cache_settings - feel free to add your own conditional kwarg (if diskcache needs one for ttl, though I don't think it does) the same way.
- Added Cache.set_compression(compress) method, Cache._encode_for_disk(value), Cache._decode_from_disk(raw) methods (all new, placed right after __init__, before get()).
- get(): changed 'response = self.disk_cache[key]' -> 'response = self._decode_from_disk(self.disk_cache[key])'. Layer your disk_cache.expire() call around/before this line as needed.
- put(): changed 'self.disk_cache[key] = value' -> 'self.disk_cache[key] = self._encode_for_disk(value)'. Layer your expire=ttl handling around this - note self._encode_for_disk returns either raw value (compress=None) or a bytes blob (compress set), so if you need expire= as a kwarg to disk_cache.set(...) instead of __setitem__, you'll need '(self.disk_cache.set(key, self._encode_for_disk(value), expire=ttl) if ttl else ...)' style - happy to adjust if that conflicts, just ping me.
- I did NOT add a module-level set_compression() function or touch the bottom of the file - Cache.set_compression is a method, so 'def request_cache(' and everything after it (including where you'll add set_ttl()) is untouched by me.

__init__.py:
- configure_cache() signature: added trailing params 'compress: Optional[str] = None, cull_limit: Optional[int] = None, eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,' right after enable_litellm_cache - append your 'ttl' after eviction_policy.
- Converted the 'dspy.cache = Cache(...)' call to keyword args and added compress=compress, cull_limit=cull_limit, eviction_policy=eviction_policy - just add ttl=ttl as another kwarg line, no positional ordering concerns.
- Did NOT touch DSPY_CACHE module-level instantiation, imports, or __all__ (no set_compression import needed since it's a Cache method) - all free for your set_ttl import/export additions.

Go ahead and layer your TTL changes now. Let me know if you hit any conflict.
